title: Index merge wrongly ignore some predicates of generated column
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```sql
CREATE TABLE t3 (
  id int PRIMARY KEY,
  c1 varchar(256),
  c2 varchar(256) GENERATED ALWAYS AS (concat(c1, c1)) VIRTUAL,
  KEY (id)
);
 
insert into t3(id, c1) values (50, 'c');

SELECT /*+ USE_INDEX_MERGE(`t3`)*/ id FROM `t3` WHERE c2 BETWEEN 'a' AND 'b' GROUP BY id HAVING id < 100 or id > 0;
```

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

```sql
Empty set
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

```sql
mysql> SELECT /*+ USE_INDEX_MERGE(`t3`)*/ id FROM `t3` WHERE c2 BETWEEN 'a' AND 'b' GROUP BY id HAVING id < 100 or id > 0;
+----+
| id |
+----+
| 50 |
+----+
1 row in set (0.01 sec)
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
poc: c2 varchar(256) GENERATED ALWAYS AS (concat(c1, c1)) VIRTUAL,
SELECT /*+ USE_INDEX_MERGE(`t3`)*/ id FROM `t3` WHERE c2 BETWEEN 'a' AND 'b' GROUP BY id HAVING id < 100 or id > 0;
key_bug_features: Index merge ignores predicates of generated column
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t4 (id int PRIMARY KEY, c1 varchar(256), c2 varchar(256) GENERATED ALWAYS AS (concat(c1, ' - ', c1)) VIRTUAL, c3 int DEFAULT NULL, KEY (c2));
SELECT /*+ USE_INDEX_MERGE(`t4`)*/ id FROM t4 WHERE c2 IS NOT NULL AND c3 BETWEEN 1 AND 3 GROUP BY id HAVING id < 5;
CREATE TABLE t5 (id int PRIMARY KEY, c1 varchar(256) NOT NULL, c2 int DEFAULT 0, c3 varchar(256) GENERATED ALWAYS AS (IFNULL(c1, '') || c2) VIRTUAL, KEY (c3));
INSERT INTO t5(id, c1, c2) VALUES (1, 'data', 1), (2, NULL, 2);
